commands.review_summary:
The branch changes Telegram isolated polling spool recovery to stop refreshing claims when drain heartbeats expire, recover live foreign claims after a 30-minute lease, and add focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +84, Tests +114. Total +198 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, without executing tests: current main refreshes `claimed_at` independent of completed drain cycles and filters live foreign claims out of stale recovery. The PR body also reports a current-main failing regression where `claimed_at` advanced while the drain was blocked.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review should decide lease/proof acceptance on the maintainer-authored draft; there is no discrete automated repair to queue.

target.title: fix(telegram): recover stalled ingress spool claims
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Telegram isolated polling can leave updates permanently claimed while the ingress spool drain is wedged. The claim refresh loop continued advancing `claimed_at` even when no drain cycle completed, and live foreign claims were excluded from stale recovery. That matches the reported `channel_ingress_events` state: pending Telegram updates accumulated while claimed rows stayed live.

## Why This Change Was Made

- Gate claim refresh on a shared successful-drain heartbeat, with fail-closed behavior after the grace window.
- Stop renewing a claim and supersede the Telegram reply fence when the drain heartbeat expires or the claim refresh is lost.
- Use an explicit 30-minute live-foreign claim lease for recovery instead of the per-handler timeout.
- Preserve current-process orphan recovery and avoid treating a reused current PID as a foreign live owner.
- Add regression coverage for a blocked drain and an expired foreign claim.

## User Impact

This affects the Telegram isolated polling ingress spool only. It does not change Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, or non-spooled Telegram webhook delivery. Stalled Telegram spool claims can now become recoverable instead of keeping the lane permanently claimed; healthy drain cycles continue refreshing active claims.

## Evidence

- Repro on current `main`: the stalled-drain regression failed because `claimed_at` advanced from `1000` to `601001` while the drain was blocked.
- Local focused proof: 91 tests passed across `polling-session`, `telegram-ingress-spool`, and `ingress-queue` suites.
- Testbox: `check:changed` passed on `tbx_01kw318gh791d7bmhk963nhfn7`; `corepack pnpm build` passed on the same box.
- Linux Crabbox: both regression cases passed in run `run_1a86842eb325` (`https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_1a86842eb325`).
- Autoreview: no accepted/actionable findings. It noted a follow-up for Telegram update types that do not register a reply fence; that broader cancellation seam is outside this scoped spool/lease fix.

AI-assisted: Codex authored and verified this patch; maintainer review requested.
target.author: vincentkoc
